{
  "weights": {

    "R1.7": 0.3,
    "RX.1": 0.7,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 0.7,
    "RX.4": 0.3,
    "RX.5": 0.7
  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.1",
    "R1.2",
    "R1.3",
    "R1.6",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Which scope should I prioritize for helping with this section: (A) high-level claim and evidence alignment plus contribution reframing, (B) sentence-level writing polish only, or (C) both, prioritizing A?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The contribution and thesis may need reframing to reflect the latency–accuracy tradeoff and mixed benchmark outcomes.",
    "R1.2": "There is an internal inconsistency between the claim of consistent improvements and the reported per-benchmark results.",
    "R1.3": "Claims about improvements and parity must be aligned with the specific WER numbers and latency metrics reported.",
    "R1.6": "Sentence-level clarity and tone can be refined to accurately convey nuanced results without ambiguity.",
    "RX.1": "Any revisions should transparently communicate uncertainty and limits of generalization from the reported tests.",
    "RX.2": "Overclaiming must be avoided given mixed performance relative to the stated baseline.",
    "RX.3": "Work should remain scoped to improving this section rather than expanding into unrelated parts of the paper.",
    "RX.4": "Avoid framing that could mislead stakeholders into risky deployment decisions based on overstated results.",
    "RX.5": "Maintain traceability from each claim to the exact benchmarks, settings, and metrics cited in the section."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Which scope should I prioritize for helping with this section: (A) high-level claim and evidence alignment plus contribution reframing, (B) sentence-level writing polish only, or (C) both, prioritizing A?

rationale.R1.1: The contribution and thesis may need reframing to reflect the latency–accuracy tradeoff and mixed benchmark outcomes.
rationale.R1.2: There is an internal inconsistency between the claim of consistent improvements and the reported per-benchmark results.
rationale.R1.3: Claims about improvements and parity must be aligned with the specific WER numbers and latency metrics reported.
rationale.R1.6: Sentence-level clarity and tone can be refined to accurately convey nuanced results without ambiguity.
rationale.RX.1: Any revisions should transparently communicate uncertainty and limits of generalization from the reported tests.
rationale.RX.2: Overclaiming must be avoided given mixed performance relative to the stated baseline.
rationale.RX.3: Work should remain scoped to improving this section rather than expanding into unrelated parts of the paper.
rationale.RX.4: Avoid framing that could mislead stakeholders into risky deployment decisions based on overstated results.
rationale.RX.5: Maintain traceability from each claim to the exact benchmarks, settings, and metrics cited in the section.
